Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Alfred E. Kahn papers
Kahn, Alfred E. (Alfred Edward.)
Ammons, A. R., 1926-2001
4055
140 cubic feet. 140 cubic feet.
1942-1994. Thesis notes and typescripts, legal records, administrative files, and other materials. Includes notes and typescripts of Kahn's Ph.D. thesis, The Position of Great Britain in the World Economy, 1942; drafts of The Economics of Regulation, 1970; records concerning his participation in natural gas regulation hearings before the Federal Power Commission, ca.1956-1970; and his participation in proceedings involving a Honolulu anti-trust case, n.d. Also material from Cornell years, including files on Economics Department, Deanship of College of Arts and Sciences, and the Kahn-Bowers Report. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 10 11 85 - folder 1 ======= 10 11 85 - folder 1 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2070447
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Date completed:
September 1994December 2014
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, July 2003Evan Earle, January 2015
Date Modified:
Kristen Reichenbach, August 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Economist, professor of economics at Cornell University.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Includes notes and typescripts of Kahn's Ph.D. thesis, The Position of Great Britain in the World Economy, 1942; drafts of The Economics of Regulation, 1970; records concerning his participation in natural gas regulation hearings before the Federal Power Commission, ca.1956-1970; and his participation in proceedings involving a Honolulu anti-trust case, n.d. Also material from Cornell years, including files on Economics Department, Deanship of College of Arts and Sciences, and the Kahn-Bowers Report.
Includes Kahn's testimony in United States District Court regarding anti-trust actions pertaining to several cereal companies.
Also, a poem, "Mountain Talk," written by Archie Ammons for Alfred Kahn and inscribed to him, August 1972. Two cartoons and a certificate.
